author: Meagan Nunez | Brand & Content Mentor
title: The ATM Strategy for Content That Converts
one_liner: Use a 90/10 value-to-sales ratio with actionable content to build trust and drive purchases.
caption:
You’re posting constantly and still not making sales. 
And this is because you’re depositing in the wrong currency.

Most creators I work with inside The Creator Collective come in making this exact mistake, giving a lot, but not giving the right thing. So when it’s time to sell, the account is empty.

The ATM analogy that changed how I think about value content, watch til the end.

If you’re ready to stop guessing and start building content that actually converts, 
The Creator Collective is open. 
Limited to 40 spots, 
join through the link on my profile 🚀

#contentcreation #personalbranding #contentstrategy
key_points: Post actionable, result-driven content (not vague tips) — these are the 'right currency' your audience can actually use | Follow a 90% value / 10% ask ratio so your audience feels indebted before you pitch | Wrong currency: generic tips like 'eat more protein'; right currency: step-by-step how-tos that create real results | When your free content already solves the problem, your paid offer becomes the obvious next step
transcript: I made $100K in a month without asking people to buy my shit. And no, I did not use reverse sales psychology or sales tactics. I just learned how ATMs work. Let me explain. It's either you're constantly posting but never selling, so you're broke and resentful. Or you're constantly selling but never converting, so you're annoying and still broke. Both extremes are f***ing you over. So let me show you how to fix that. I stopped thinking about content as value posts versus sales posts and started thinking about it like a bank account. Imagine you're at an ATM in the US. You deposit US dollars. The machine reads that you can withdraw it later. It works. But what if you started depositing Japanese Yen, Euros, Monopoly money? They might have value somewhere, but this ATM can't read it, so it spits it back out. Yes, you made a deposit, but it doesn't count. That's what most of you are doing with your content. You're making the deposits, but it's in the wrong currency. You're posting tips that sound good, but doesn't really get anyone results. So when you finally ask for the sale, your account stays empty. So the question here is, what is the right currency and what deposits actually count? First is value that gets results, not tips or inspirational posts. These are actionable and tangible steps that people can start implementing today and actually see a change. The wrong currency looks like, here's 10 tips to lose weight. Number one, progressive overload. Number two, eat more protein. Number three, eat your veggies. That doesn't do anything, okay? You're presenting concepts that your ideal audience don't really know what to do instead of steps that they can start doing. Now, here are example of content pieces I've made that was valuable for my audience. First is a form correction video. I didn't tell them, do this exercise. I showed them how to do it. Second is for nutrition. I didn't just tell them to start dieting, eat less food. I showed them how to weigh their food, how to calculate their calories and their macros, and so on and so forth. Those are deposits that count. And that is a currency this ATM accepts. Now, how many times should you be posting before you make a sale? Here's my rule. 90% of the time, give so much value, and then 10% do an ask. The reason why I follow this ratio is because you want to give so much value to your audience that it makes them feel like, damn, I am robbing Megan right now. What is she offering? Take my credit card. I need your help. That's exactly what happened. I posted so much value when it comes to losing body fat, how to train in the gym, how to fuel their body better, that once I launched my digital product on solving that problem, it gave me an abundance of withdrawal. This is how you create content that doesn't feel like a sales pitch. Your offer, once you do launch it, just becomes the obvious choice for them because you've already solved their problem with your free content. Now, start abundantly making the right deposits so eventually you can abundantly withdraw. You're welcome.

author: kyla scanlon
title: AI Hype Masks Real Market Risks
one_liner: Markets hit record highs on AI speculation while ignoring Fed independence threats and systemic risks.
caption: April 15 2026: ArtificialInteligenceBird + continued Fed pressure
key_points: Allbirds pivoted to 'New Bird AI' with a vague $50M deal, stock surged 600% — the investor's $3/share conversion became worth $300M+ | This mirrors blockchain-name-change era: investors are betting on market hype around AI, not actual AI businesses | S&P 500 hit an all-time high within 11 days of a 5–10% drop — fastest recovery since 1928, signaling reflexive dip-buying | Trump threatening to fire Powell and politicize the Fed poses serious systemic risk the market is largely ignoring
transcript: the stock market keeps on hitting record highs and there's a really good example of why that happened today and there's also a really good example of why this shouldn't be happening that happened today. So since 1928, this is the first time the S&P 500 has hit a new all-time high within 11 days of falling five to 10%. That is a market that is trained to buy every single dip no matter what. And you can see the market's excitement in things like Allbirds. So they're a sneaker company that IPOed at a $4 billion valuation in 2021. They just sold for about $40 million and announced that they are pivoting to AI compute infrastructure, calling themselves New Bird AI. The stock went up almost 600%, which is super normal. Allbirds was basically a dead company as Matt Levine of Bloomberg Opinion wrote about. So some investor shows up and says, I'm gonna give you a $50 million to pivot to AI, but I'm only giving you $5 million now. This investor will decide later whether or not to put in the other $45 million and they'll get the right to convert their loan into stock at $3 a share. So Allbirds announces this AI pivot that's not very clear how it's going to happen. The stock rockets to $19. So now that investor's $3 per share conversion is absurdly valuable. $50 million of notes converts into shares worth over $300 million at the current price. The investor had other great clauses baked in that made sure they made a lot of money. And as Matt Levine points out, this is a great portfolio strategy. So if you have 10 dead companies, you put up $5 million each, $50 million total, most of the companies will fall. If one holds on to hype around AI or whatever long enough, you convert, you sell, you make a ton of money and you cover everything else. It is the market's response to AI that you're investing in, not the AI itself, right? It's very similar to when an IC company switched to have blockchain in their name. So the market's so excited, but everything is pointing to the market not being excited. Trump went on Fox Business this morning and said that he'll fire Jerome Powell if Powell doesn't leave when his term as Fed chair ends on May 15th. Powell said he's not leaving until the investigation on him and the Federal Reserve is well and truly over with transparency and finality. There's a lot of danger of Trump's Federal Reserve becoming politicized. Trump very much wants the Federal Reserve to lower interest rates. That is not good for the current economy that we have. And there's a worry that Kevin Walsh would do that because there's pressure politically to do so. Janet Yellen, former chair of the Federal Reserve and former treasury secretary says she's never seen a threat of this level to the Federal Reserve. And if the Fed does lose independence, that's when things become really, really dangerous because of all of a sudden, the Federal Reserve is setting rates due to politics. You do not have a healthy economy. So the market is freaking out about an AI shoe company when there's clear systemic risks that they're not pricing very well, both in terms of the war and what could happen with the war and what's happening with the Federal Reserve. This is a market that really just wants to focus on the fun stuff rather than dealing with reality.

author: Jordan Yeoh
title: Fix Your Ab Exercises for Better Results
one_liner: Simple form tweaks to target abs more effectively and reduce lower back strain.
caption: Simplest way to level up your Abs workout
key_points: Replace leg raises with reverse crunches to hit lower abs and spare the lower back | Support your neck with hands during crunches to eliminate neck pain and fully engage abs | Open your knees when crunching upward to maximize ab activation and minimize hip flexor/lower back involvement
transcript: When you do leg raises, you might feel a lot on your lower back. But when you do reverse crunches, more lower abs, lesser lower back. When you do your crunches like this, you'll get neck pain. But when you place your hand behind your neck and crunch just like this, you'll use fully abs. When you go all the way up, that's hip flexor and lower back. But when you open up your knees and crunch through all the way up, you use a lot of abs, lesser lower back.

author: Chris Lee
title: Napa Soup Dumpling Lasagna Recipe
one_liner: A low-carb, high-protein lasagna using napa cabbage instead of wonton wrappers, with beef and shrimp filling.
caption:
Napa Soup Dumpling Lasagna

470 Calories
63gP | 10gC | 19gF

Ingredients:
150g Extra Lean Beef (Raw weight)
150g Shrimp (Raw weight)
Chopped Green Onion
1 Tsp Grated Ginger
½ Tbsp Minced Garlic
1 Tbsp Soy Sauce
½ Tbsp Sesame Oil
½ Tbsp Oyster Sauce
1 Tsp Brown Splenda
½  Tsp Salt
1 Tsp White Pepper
¼ Cup Chicken Broth
Napa Cabbage
Sesame Seeds

Sesame Oil Soy Sauce:
2 Tbsp of soy sauce
½ Tsp of sesame oil
Pinch of chili flakes/gochugaru

Instructions:
1. Into a bowl combine the following ingredients: 150g Extra Lean Beef (Raw weight), 150g Shrimp (Raw weight), Chopped Green Onion, 1 Tsp Grated Ginger, ½ Tbsp Garlic, 1 Tbsp Soy Sauce, ½ Tbsp Sesame Oil, ½ Tbsp Oyster Sauce, 1 Tsp Brown Splenda, ½ Tsp Salt, 1 Tsp White Pepper and mix.
2. Into an oven safe dish, line the bottom with napa cabbage then place about ½ of the filling mixture and flatten out. Place napa cabbage until completely covered. Repeat with a layer of filling and top with another layer of napa cabbage.
3.  Add about ¼ cup of chicken stock and steam until the mixture is cooked through (took around 10-12mins).
4. Top with green onions, sesame seeds, soy sauce mixture and enjoy!

Follow for daily healthy recipes!
key_points: 63g protein, only 10g carbs, and 470 calories per serving | Replaces wonton wrappers with layered napa cabbage for a low-carb twist | Filling combines lean beef, shrimp, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, and oyster sauce | Steamed in an oven-safe dish for 10-12 minutes and topped with sesame soy sauce
transcript: Ditch the wonton wrappers if you're trying to lose weight because you need to try this Napa Soup Dumpling Lasagna. It packs 63 grams of protein for only 470 calories. It has only 10 grams of carbs and packs so much flavor for something so simple and easy to make. Full recipes in the description. And if you like recipes like these, follow me for daily healthy recipes. Enjoy!
recipe_ingredients: 150g extra lean beef (raw weight) | 150g shrimp (raw weight) | Chopped green onion | 1 tsp grated ginger | ½ tbsp minced garlic | 1 tbsp soy sauce | ½ tbsp sesame oil | ½ tbsp oyster sauce | 1 tsp brown Splenda | ½ tsp salt | 1 tsp white pepper | ¼ cup chicken broth | Napa cabbage leaves | Sesame seeds | 2 tbsp soy sauce (for sauce) | ½ tsp sesame oil (for sauce) | Pinch of chili flakes/gochugaru (for sauce)
recipe_instructions: 1. Combine beef, shrimp, green onion, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, sesame oil, oyster sauce, brown Splenda, salt, and white pepper in a bowl and mix well. | 2. Line the bottom of an oven-safe dish with napa cabbage, add half the filling and flatten. Cover with napa cabbage, repeat with remaining filling, and top with a final layer of napa cabbage. | 3. Add ¼ cup chicken broth and steam until cooked through, about 10-12 minutes. | 4. Mix 2 tbsp soy sauce, ½ tsp sesame oil, and a pinch of chili flakes for the sauce. Top dish with green onions, sesame seeds, and the sauce before serving.

author: Claire Beluga
title: Close the Gap Between Deciding and Doing
one_liner: The speed between intention and action is the single most reliable predictor of extraordinary vs. ordinary life.
caption:
The distance between deciding and doing by Derek Sivers. 

The distance between deciding and doing is the single most reliable predictor of whether your lite will be extraordinary or ordinary. Not talent, not circumstances, not even the quality of your decisions-but how quickly you collapse the space between intention and reality. Think of this gap as a kind of friction coefficient on your existence: the smaller it is, the more of your internal force actually translates into external motion. When you can move from “I should do this” to physically doing it within hours instead of weeks, you’re not just accomplishing more-you’re operating in a fundamentally different mode of being where your thoughts have immediate consequences in the world, where your inner life and outer life are in a constant, tight, conversation.

#productivity #failure #selfhelp
key_points: Shrinking the gap between decision and execution increases iteration speed — 10 attempts vs. 1 | Faster action means faster feedback, faster failure, and faster course-correction | Hiding in intention without action is self-betrayal; reality only responds to what you actually do | True success is proof you lived in sync with your values — your knowing self and acting self unified
transcript: The distance between deciding and doing is the single largest predictor of whether your life will be extraordinary or ordinary. Some of the biggest doers in my life have sent this to me. By doers, I mean people who get shit done. This is their Bible. You will become a doer after hearing this. The distance between deciding and doing is the single most reliable predictor of whether your life will be extraordinary or ordinary. Not talent, not circumstance, not even the quality of your decisions, but how quickly you collapse the space between intention and reality. Think of this gap as a kind of friction coefficient on your existence. The smaller it is, the more of your internal force actually translates into external motion. When you can move from I should do this to physically doing it within hours instead of weeks, you're not just accomplishing more, you're operating in a fundamentally different mode of being where your thoughts have immediate consequences on the world, where your inner life and outer life are in constant, tight conversation. This matters profoundly because success isn't really about outcomes. It's about iteration speed. The person who can decide and act in the same breath gets 10 attempts at something while someone with a week-long gap between decision and execution gets one. They fail faster, learn faster, course-correct faster, and compound their advantages faster. More crucially, they remain in contact with reality. When your decisions meet the world immediately, you get immediate feedback about whether they were good decisions. You can't hide in comfortable delusions about what you would or could do because you're constantly discovering what you actually do. This brutal, liberating honesty with reality is what separates people who achieve extraordinary things from people who have extraordinary intentions. Such a banger. The ultimate insight is that shrinking this gap isn't just correlated with success. It might be the most honest definition of success we have because what is success except proof that you actually lived according to your own values and vision, that you were who you said you were? Every moment between decision and action is a moment of self-betrayal, a small death where a possible version of you fails to be born. Successful people have built lives with almost no lag between recognition and response, between seeing what needs doing and doing it. They've achieved something most people never do. They've synchronized their two selves, the one who knows and the one who acts, into a single undivided person moving through the world with uncommon force.

author: theChefoutWest

Fudgy high-protein chocolate cheesecake

author: Steven Bartlett
title: Your Brain Fights Every Diet You Try
one_liner: Neuroscientist explains how the brain actively resists weight loss by increasing hunger and lowering metabolism.
caption: Weight Loss Scientist, Dr Giles Yeo, says our brain HATES us losing weight! 🧠🤔
key_points: Even losing just 5 lbs triggers the brain to treat it as a survival threat | The brain fights back by increasing hunger and secretly lowering your metabolism | Eating the same calories, you now store more than you burn during weight loss | When you stop any diet, the brain pulls weight back up — making regain nearly inevitable
transcript: Everyone's brain hates it when they lose weight. It doesn't matter your starting point. You could start from a point where you are athletic versus someone who's not athletic, couch potato phenotype. The moment you lose a little bit of weight, we're talking even a few pounds. I'm not even talking, you're talking about a stone. Even if you lost five pounds, what happens in your brain is your brain is used to you carrying, you or me carrying a certain amount of weight. The moment your weight starts to go down as an adult, it goes, this is a big flag comes up. This is reducing my chance of survival. This is what the brain thinks. And so what it does is it begins to use strategies, not conscious, nothing to do with our brain, anything like that, to drag us back up, kicking and screaming to where we were before. First of all, it makes us hungry. And second, it actually very, very secretively lowers your metabolism ever so slightly. Even eating exactly the same thing, you are now storing more than you're burning. Even eating exactly the same thing. Part of the strategy to get you back up to where you were before. So once you were on that keto diet, and we can debate, talk about how it works and whether it's useful, but once you're on the diet, your weight goes off, you're able to keep it, but you said, man, I can't do this. And so you stop. And the moment you stop, your brain goes, comes back on and starts dragging you back up. This is going to be true for pretty much every single diet that is out there. The moment you stop the diet, the weight will come back on.

author: TED
title: How Language Shapes How You Think
one_liner: The language you speak influences your perception, memory, and how you interpret the world around you.
caption: The language you use — whether it’s English, Chinese or French — shapes your experience of the world. Here’s how: http://t.ted.com/hIZAIlv
key_points: Kuuk Thaayorre speakers use cardinal directions instead of left/right, giving them superior spatial orientation | Grammatical gender in languages shapes how speakers describe objects (e.g., German vs. Spanish views of bridges) | Spanish speakers omit the agent in accident descriptions, making them less likely to blame individuals | Language trains attention — different languages make speakers notice different details about the same events
transcript: And does the language we speak shape the way we think? These are the Kuktayer people. They live in Pohnpurao, at the very west edge of Cape York. In Kuktayer, they don't use words like left and right. And instead, everything is in cardinal directions, north, south, east and west. And when I say everything, I really mean everything. You would say something like, oh, there's an ant on your southwest leg. Or move your cup to the north-northeast a little bit. In fact, the way that you say hello in Kuktayer is, which way are you going? And the answer should be, north-northeast in the far distance, how about you? People who speak languages like this stay oriented really, really well. They stay oriented better than we used to think humans could. We used to think that humans were worse than other creatures because some biological excuse. Oh, we don't have magnets in our beaks or in our scales. No. If your language and your culture trains you to do it, actually, you can do it. Lots of languages have grammatical gender. So every noun gets assigned a gender, often masculine or feminine, and these genders differ across languages. Could this have any consequence for how people think? Actually, it turns out that's the case. So if you ask German and Spanish speakers to, say, describe a bridge, like the one here, a bridge happens to be grammatically feminine in German, grammatically masculine in Spanish, German speakers are more likely to say bridges are beautiful, elegant, these stereotypically feminine words, whereas Spanish speakers will be more likely to say they're strong or long, these masculine words. Languages also differ in how they describe events. In English, it's fine to say, he broke the vase. In a language like Spanish, you might be more likely to say, the vase broke or the vase broke itself. If it's an accident, you wouldn't say that someone did it. In English, quite weirdly, we can even say things like, I broke my arm. Now, in lots of languages, you couldn't use that construction unless you are a lunatic and you went out looking to break your arm and you succeeded. People who speak different languages will pay attention to different things depending on what their language usually requires them to do. And that gives you the opportunity to ask, why do I think the way that I do? How could I think differently? And also, what thoughts do I wish to create? Learn English for free www.engvid.com

author: TWS Training
title: Hang Longer to Fix Your Shoulders
one_liner: Consistent bar hanging can reshape the acromion bone and eliminate most shoulder pain and pinching.
caption:
Hanging can fix your shoulders… but only if you hang long enough for this to happen… 

Most shoulder problems come down to one thing:

👉 Lack of space

That’s why overhead movements start to pinch.

And a big reason for that is a small bone called the acromion.

Over time, gravity and the weight of your arm slowly pull on it… and it can start to hook downward.

Less space = more pinching and pain.

But hanging does the exact opposite.

Just like braces can reshape your teeth over time… your shoulder works the same way.

If you hang long enough and consistently, it can help guide that bone back toward its flatter, natural shape.

We’re talking millimeters…

But those millimeters make a huge difference.

⚠️ But here’s the catch:

This only works if you hang long enough.

And yes, you can hang bare-handed.

But if you can barely hang on… your shoulders never get the time they actually need to adapt.

Because the real magic isn’t just hanging longer… 

It’s being able to fully relax into the hang

And this is where grip assist can go a long way.

Don’t get me wrong, I’m all for grip strength.

But when it comes to fixing my shoulders, my priority is simple:

👉 More hang time
👉 More relaxed shoulders
👉 Easy enough to do every day

All and all, what really matters is consistancy. 

And if you want to check out the grips I’m using the link is in my profile.
key_points: The acromion bone hooks downward over time due to posture and arm weight, reducing shoulder space and causing pinching | Hanging from a bar imposes enough force to gradually reshape the acromion back toward its original flat position | Benefits kick in past the 1-minute mark — consistency and duration matter more than grip strength alone | Over 90% of Dr. John Kirsch's patients avoided shoulder surgery by adding regular hanging to their routine
transcript: Most people have no idea what hanging is actually doing to their shoulders. Everyone thinks it's just stretching the shoulders and decompressing the spine. And while that's true, the real magic lies in that it can actually help reshape the part of the scapula responsible for most shoulder problems. Now the bone I'm referring to is called the acromion and it's actually part of our scapula and because of our terrible posture and believe it or not, the weight of our arms dragging us down for many years has actually caused that acromion to hook and it's run out of usable space in our shoulders. So things like our bicep tendons and our ligaments, whenever we put our hands above our head, they actually fray against that bone and cause all kinds of pain and pinching symptoms. Now the really cool thing about hanging is, is that when Dr. John Kirsch discovered it as a solution to these shoulder problems, what he found was that when you hang from a pull-up bar, you can actually impose a force great enough that can actually start reshaping that acromion to its original position and gain the necessary millimeters of space because you don't need much space to be able to restore full function in most of his patients. He found that over 90% of them actually got to avoid shoulder surgery simply by introducing hanging. Now the only catch to this is that you've got to learn to hang long enough. Now I'm all for grip strength, but if your shoulders are completely busted, you're going to want to use some kind of assistance because the real benefits of hanging happen when you can start clocking past that one minute marker because if you can get past one minute and you can do it frequently and consistently enough every single week, not only are you going to improve the state of your shoulders, but you'll actually get to relax into it and as we've seen, will help reshape that acromion bone back into its straight position and you should be able to get rid of any kind of shoulder problems that you might have. All in all, hanging, it's an excellent exercise. You just need to make sure you hang long enough to actually see the benefits.

author: Wendy the Food Scientist
title: Crispy Lentil Crackers: No Oil, All Crunch
one_liner: Make high-protein, high-fiber lentil crackers using just lentils, water, and spices — no oil needed.
caption:
Crispy, high protein, high fiber crackers — no frying, no oil.
By @wendythefoodscientist

Just lentils, water and spices. That's it.
Here's the food science behind why it works: when soaked lentils are blended, their starches gelatinize in the oven and then dehydrate — forming a thin, glassy crunch. No oil needed. The crispiness comes from the starch, not the fat.
Spread thin, bake twice, and you get a snap that sounds like a proper cracker.
Use any lentil, any seeds, any spices. New batch every week.

Ingredients
125g / ⅔ cup dry lentils
100ml / ⅓ cup + 1 tbsp water
30g / 3 tbsp sesame seeds
½ tsp salt
¼ tsp dried rosemary

Method
Soak lentils in cold water for 8-12 hours. Drain and rinse well.
Add soaked lentils and water to a high-speed blender. Blend until completely smooth — no lumps remaining.
Add sesame seeds, salt and rosemary. Stir to combine.
Spread as thinly as possible onto a lined baking tray.
Bake at 180°C / 355°F for 20 minutes.
Remove from oven and cut into squares or rectangles.
Return to oven and bake for a further 30-35 minutes until golden, dry and crispy.
Allow to cool completely before serving — they crisp up further as they cool.

#lentil #highprotein #veganrecipe #vegan #healthyvegan #plantbased
key_points: Soaked lentils blended smooth form the base — starch gelatinizes then dehydrates in the oven for crispiness | Spread batter as thin as possible, bake twice: 20 min first, then 30-35 min after slicing | Fully customizable — use any lentil, seeds, herbs, or spices | Cheaper and healthier alternative to store-bought protein snacks
transcript: How to make high-fiber, high-protein lentil crackers. They turn out super crispy without any frying, all thanks to the starches in lentils. It's so easy to make, you never have to buy expensive protein snacks again. You can choose any lentil, herbs and spices you like. The trick is to spread it thin, bake until set but soft enough to slice. Then bake again until super crispy.
recipe_ingredients: 125g / ⅔ cup dry lentils | 100ml / ⅓ cup + 1 tbsp water | 30g / 3 tbsp sesame seeds | ½ tsp salt | ¼ tsp dried rosemary
recipe_instructions: Soak lentils in cold water for 8–12 hours, then drain and rinse well. | Blend soaked lentils and water in a high-speed blender until completely smooth. | Stir in sesame seeds, salt, and rosemary. | Spread mixture as thinly as possible onto a lined baking tray. | Bake at 180°C / 355°F for 20 minutes. | Remove from oven and cut into squares or rectangles. | Return to oven and bake for a further 30–35 minutes until golden, dry, and crispy. | Allow to cool completely before serving — they crisp up further as they cool.

author: Wendy the Food Scientist
title: Soak Nuts for the Crispiest Granola Ever
one_liner: Soaking nuts and seeds before baking removes bitter tannins and creates a crispier, more porous granola texture.
caption:
Why soak your nuts and seeds for the best granola ever
By @wendythefoodscientist

I’ve been doing this for a long time but rarely see it shared online, so here’s my secret: soaking nuts and seeds before making granola.
Why?
1. Cleaner flavor: Soaking + rinsing leaches some bitter tannins (especially in walnuts/sunflower seed), so the nutty taste is smooth and clean.
2. Crispier bite: Soaking improves texture because the nuts and seeds swell, and after drying it is more brittle, more porous. The result is super crispy.
When to use it?
For granola, roasted nuts, or any other recipe where you want extra crispy and tasty nuts/seeds
How to soak?
Rule of thumb
- Almonds: 8–12 h
- Walnuts, pecan, pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds: 4–6 h
- Cashews: 2–4 h
- Sesame seeds: optional, 1-2 h
Rinse, soak in salted water (1.5-2 tsp per L), rinse again

The nutritional benefits of “activated” nuts are debated. While soaking may reduce some antinutrients, the enzymes activated during soaking don’t survive high-heat baking. If enzyme activity matters to you, dehydrate at low temperatures ≤50–60°C (122-140°F) instead.
The reason I soak is for texture and flavor. This method delivers the crispiest, most delicious granola I’ve ever made. I have developed a method that can be done with any oven.

Bake soaked nuts and seeds 130°C / 265°F 30-40min until dry and lightly toasted
175°C / 350°F 15-20min until roasted.

#healthyfood #healthyeating #cookinghack
key_points: Soaking leaches bitter tannins (especially from walnuts and sunflower seeds), resulting in a cleaner, smoother flavor | Nuts swell during soaking, then become more porous and brittle after drying — making granola extra crispy | Soak times vary: almonds 8–12h, walnuts/pecans/pumpkin/sunflower seeds 4–6h, cashews 2–4h; always use salted water (1.5–2 tsp per liter) | Bake soaked nuts at 130°C (265°F) for 30–40 min to dry, then 175°C (350°F) for 15–20 min to roast
transcript: This is my secret to the best granola you've ever tasted. Perfectly crispy and tasty in 5 simple steps. Rinse your nuts and seeds then soak them to draw out soluble bitter compounds like tannins. After soaking, rinse again to wash away what's been released. Then bake at low temperature until dry to the touch. Lastly, add the rest of the granola ingredients and bake until golden. It gets even crispier as it cools when you can hear it crackling in the pan. Most recipes skip the soaking step but trust me, this is what takes your granola to the next level.
recipe_ingredients: nuts and seeds of choice (almonds, walnuts, pecans, pumpkin seeds, sunflower seeds, cashews, sesame seeds) | 1.5–2 tsp salt per 1 liter water (for soaking) | remaining granola ingredients (oats, sweetener, oil, etc.) as desired
recipe_instructions: 1. Rinse nuts and seeds thoroughly | 2. Soak in salted water (1.5–2 tsp salt per liter): almonds 8–12h, walnuts/pecans/pumpkin/sunflower seeds 4–6h, cashews 2–4h, sesame seeds 1–2h (optional) | 3. Rinse soaked nuts and seeds again to wash away released tannins | 4. Spread on a baking tray and bake at 130°C (265°F) for 30–40 minutes until dry to the touch | 5. Add remaining granola ingredients, then bake at 175°C (350°F) for 15–20 minutes until golden | 6. Let cool in the pan — granola will crackle and crisp further as it cools

author: Keto Snackz
title: High-Protein Blueberry Cheesecake
one_liner: A 6-ingredient baked blueberry cheesecake using cottage cheese for extra protein.
caption:
Healthy High Protein Blueberry Cheesecake 🍰😋🫐 

Ingredients: 
3 tbsp cottage cheese
2 oz cream cheese
1 large egg
2 tbsp sweetener (I used monkfruit)
2 tbsp blueberries
1 tsp vanilla extract

Instructions:
1. Blend all ingredients except the blueberries and pour it in a greased dish.
2. Add your blueberries & bake @ 325F for 30-35 mins. 
3. Take it out, chill for a couple hours & enjoy!

Pro tip: Mash some extra blueberries and swirl them in the top with a toothpick👌
key_points: Blend cottage cheese, cream cheese, egg, sweetener, and vanilla; pour into a greased dish | Add blueberries on top and bake at 325°F for 30–35 minutes | Chill for a couple hours before serving | Pro tip: mash extra blueberries and swirl on top with a toothpick
transcript: If you bake blueberries with cottage cheese, an egg, cream cheese, and sweetener for 30 minutes, you get a healthy blueberry cheesecake.
recipe_ingredients: 3 tbsp cottage cheese | 2 oz cream cheese | 1 large egg | 2 tbsp sweetener (monk fruit) | 2 tbsp blueberries | 1 tsp vanilla extract
recipe_instructions: Blend all ingredients except the blueberries and pour into a greased dish. | Add blueberries on top and bake at 325°F for 30–35 minutes. | Take out, chill for a couple hours, and serve. | Optional: mash extra blueberries and swirl on top with a toothpick before baking.

author: Brycen Wood
title: One File Gets Your Business Into ChatGPT
one_liner: A llms.txt file on your website can get ChatGPT to recommend your business for free.
caption:
ChatGPT is recommending my vehicle wrap shop to people searching for wraps in Utah. I didn’t pay for it. I set it up with one file.

It’s called llms.txt. It sits on your website and tells AI assistants what your business does, where you are, and why someone should choose you. It took me 5 minutes to create with Claude Code.

Most businesses don’t know it exists.

My website also went from 7 visitors a day to 150 after I rebuilt it. No ads. No agency. I’ll show you how.

How long before every business has one of these?
